highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 brings Skia m151, broad performance gains, and WASM Emscripten 5.0.6 support.
highlights_body: The release merges all upstream Skia m151 bug-fix syncs, cuts allocations across HarfBuzz, SKShaper, and colour-parsing hot paths, and adds the Emscripten 5.0.6 native libraries needed for .NET 11 WASM.

bullets.detail: Three rounds of upstream chrome/m151 bug-fix merges keep the bundled Skia engine current with Google's latest stable fixes.

bullets.lead: ReadOnlySpan<char> overloads for SKColor hex parsing
bullets.detail: SKColor hex parsing is now faster and gains ReadOnlySpan<char> overloads so callers can avoid intermediate string allocations.
Removed an unnecessary unsafe modifier from GRContextOptions, making the type usable without unsafe blocks.
Restored SK_ENABLE_LEGACY_SHADERCONTEXT to resolve a CPU raster regression that appeared after the m151 engine update.
Fixed a native handle leak when calling SKImage.FromEncodedData(SKData, SKRectI) with a bounds rectangle.
Adds prebuilt Emscripten 5.0.6 static libraries and rebuilds the WASM target with the new exnref exception-handling model required by .NET 11.

contributor_summaries.ramezgerges: Added the WASM Emscripten 5.0.6 static libraries, ported the Skia submodule bump that drops the SK_BUILD_FOR_WASM redefinition, and contributed HarfBuzz allocation-free improvements.

preview_summaries.v4.151.0-preview.1.1: Preview 1 landed the Skia m151 engine update, the first round of upstream bug-fix merges, and the Skia submodule bump to drop the SK_BUILD_FOR_WASM redefinition.
preview_summaries.v4.151.0-preview.2.1: Preview 2 added broad performance improvements — allocation-free HarfBuzz UTF-8 encoding and Tag parsing, zero-copy SKShaper glyph spans, and optimised SKColor hex parsing — plus WASM Emscripten 5.0.6 support, the CPU raster regression fix, and HarfBuzz 14.2.1.

harfbuzz_summary: Updates HarfBuzzSharp to 14.2.1, eliminates per-call allocations in Tag.Parse, and makes Buffer.AddUtf8(string) allocation-free via pooled UTF-8 encoding.
